page: Amazon Web Services r3.4xlarge is a memory-optimized third-generation server instance powered by the Xen hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon E5-2670 v2 processor running at 2.5 GHz. This dedicated CPU configuration provides 16 virtual CPUs derived from 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor includes 25 MB of L3 cache and supports AVX and AES instruction sets, though it lacks newer extensions such as AVX-512 or AMX.

The instance is equipped with 122.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 7.62 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, it bundles a 320 GB NVMe SSD, providing high-speed local data access. Networking capabilities include a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps, and the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is generally weak, as evidenced by bottom 25% results in stress-ng single-core and a poor bottom 10% rating in Geekbench single-core tests. However, multi-core execution scales to average levels, with stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU scores all falling within the middle 50% category. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks also perform in the average range.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and local NVMe storage, the r3.4xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive legacy workloads that do not require modern CPU instruction sets. It is well-suited for memory-cached databases, multi-threaded data processing, and static web serving. It is less suitable for single-threaded tasks or modern machine learning workloads, as demonstrated by poor prompt processing speeds in LLM benchmarks.
description: Amazon Web Services r3.4xlarge is a memory-optimized third-generation instance utilizing the Xen hypervisor and an x86_64 Intel Xeon E5-2670 v2 processor. It delivers 16 vCPUs across 8 physical cores at 2.5 GHz, paired with 122.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 320 GB NVMe SSD. While single-core benchmarks, such as Geekbench and stress-ng, place the instance in the lower performance tiers, its multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities consistently reach average levels. The instance features a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps and lacks complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Qualitatively, the r3.4xlarge provides a resource-dense profile for memory-heavy workloads. It is best suited for databases, multi-threaded data processing, and static web serving, though it presents a performance tradeoff for single-threaded operations and modern instruction-heavy tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r3.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 122.0 GB of RAM, and a 320 GB NVMe SSD, delivering average multi-core performance for database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r3.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 16 vCPUs, 122.0 GB RAM, and a 320 GB NVMe SSD for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, substantial RAM capacity, and integrated local NVMe SSD storage.

122.0 GB of system memory optimized for memory-intensive applications
Bundled 320 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Average multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Suitable for databases, multi-threaded processing, and static web serving
